prompt: A scientist won a Nobel Prize in Physics in 1921 and is famous for the theory of relativity. In which city was this person born?
The author of 'The Origin of Species' is buried in a famous abbey in London. Name that abbey.
Consider the country whose flag is a red circle on a white background, sometimes called the Land of the Rising Sun. What is the currency of that country?
A programming language created by Guido van Rossum and first released in 1991 is named after a British comedy group. What is the name of that comedy group?
The tallest mountain on Earth measured from sea level is located on the border between two countries. Name both countries.
A film directed by the same person who directed 'Jaws' (1975) features a paleontologist and dinosaurs cloned from amber-preserved DNA. What is the title of that film?
Identify the element with atomic number 79, then state how many protons an atom of that element has.
The first programmer is often considered to be a 19th-century mathematician who worked on Charles Babbage's Analytical Engine. A programming language was named in her honor. Name that language.
